metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4192
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Cntnap1-/- mice show increased dendritic spine density in cortical layers 2/3 by Golgi staining.

Description in Figure 7 legend.
Figure 7
Cntnap1-/- mice showed increased spine density and an increase in the number of PV+ and VIP+ neurons.
(A) Representative Golgi staining of cortical layers II/III of adult Cntnap1-/- mice (apical dendrites, 5 months of age, n = 3 for WT and Cntnap1-/- mice). Scale bar: 4 μm. ....... (D) Quantitative analysis of spine density in Cntnap1-/- mice (22 and 25 neurons from each group were randomly selected and measured). Statistical significance was evaluated by Student's t test. **P = 0.0093. Data are shown as mean ± SEM.

28/01/2021 Dnyanada: The term "regulation of synapse maturation" was chosen based on the following author's comment.
Literal:
Dendritic spines are a major site of synaptic contacts. Through Golgi staining, we found that the spine density of cortical neurons was increased in Cntnap1-/- mice in comparison with WT mice, indicating that Cntnap1 deficiency leads to an abnormal synapse development in vivo.. Experimental description: Methods; Animals section
Cntnap1-KO mice were generated as previously described (47).
